title: Clean Fuels Asks EPA to Sustain Robust Biomass-based Diesel Volumes in 2026-27 RFS
content: Clean Fuels Alliance America filed comments on the Environmental Protection Agencyâ€™s Proposed Renewable Fuel Standards for 2026 and 2027. Clean Fuels expressed appreciation for EPAâ€™s intent to provide consistent RFS growth in recognition of U.S. farmersâ€™, feedstock providersâ€™, and biomass-based diesel producersâ€™ investments in new capacity. EPA proposes a robust step-change in biomass-based diesel and advanced biofuel volumes beginning in 2026. The agency projects the requirement to reach 5.61 billion gallons in 2026 â€“ more than two billion gallons higher than in 2025. According to an EPA fact sheet, â€œThese volume requirements, if finalized, will protect investments made by American corn and soybean growers, oilseed processors, and biodiesel and renewable diesel producers, whose products are critical to our countryâ€™s energy security and keeping rural economies strong.â€
author: Analysts Account
